description: Late blight is a destructive disease of tomato capable of spreading rapidly during cool, wet, and highly humid weather.
symptoms: Large irregular water-soaked or pale green leaf lesions
Lesions rapidly turn brown or black
White pathogen growth may appear around lesion margins under humid conditions
Dark lesions may develop on stems
Fruit may develop firm greasy-looking brown lesions
Severe infections can rapidly kill foliage

favorable_conditions.leaf_wetness: Prolonged wetness creates high disease risk
favorable_conditions.additional_conditions: Cool, moist, cloudy weather can support rapid disease development

spread: Nearby infected tomato or potato plants
risk_factors: Nearby infected potato or tomato crops
prevention: Monitor crops frequently during high-risk weather
Use resistant cultivars where locally appropriate

immediate_actions: Inspect surrounding tomato and potato plants
biological_control: Biological fungicides may provide supplementary preventive protection where registered but may be insufficient during severe outbreaks

chemical_control.recommended: Use locally registered late-blight fungicides according to disease forecasts and local agricultural recommendations.
chemical_control.warning: Resistance management is essential. Product availability and registration vary by region.

severity_guidance.low: Any credible suspected infection should trigger immediate monitoring and confirmation.
severity_guidance.moderate: Multiple lesions or plants affected require prompt integrated management.
severity_guidance.high: Rapidly spreading foliage or stem infection is an urgent outbreak requiring expert guidance.

farmer_advice: Late blight can spread extremely quickly. AgriForge should generate a high-priority alert when visual symptoms coincide with cool, wet, highly humid weather.
disclaimer: AI predictions alone should not be used as definitive confirmation of late blight.
